box.content: Zero Hydration, Pure Performance.

list.text: Qwik: The Resumable Meta-framework.
Express: The Standard for Node.js Backends.

box.content: Aravind Govindhasamy

list.text: Elite Technical Architect
Performance & Architecture Lead

box.content: Beyond Hydration: Why Qwik matters.
Traditional frameworks 'hydrate' on the client, essentially re-running the app to make it interactive.

list.text: Slow: High JS execution on startup.
Expensive: Downloading code that might never run.

box.content: Qwik 'pauses' execution on the server and 'resumes' it on the client without downloading or executing JS upfront.

list.text: Zero Hydration: No upfront execution cost.
Instant TTI: App is interactive as soon as the HTML arrives.

box.content: Qwik's performance is constant relative to the size of the application. More components don't mean slower starts.

list.text: Scalable: Perfect for massive e-commerce sites.
Predictable: Performance remains elite as the app grows.

box.content: The Magic behind Late Execution.
Qwik Resolution Locator. A string that identifies how to lazy-load and execute code on demand.
box.code:
// 컴포넌트가 아닌 지연 로딩 가능한 참조
const onButtonClick = $(() => console.log('Clicked!'));

box.content: Defined with a trailing dollar sign, these components are automatically split by the optimizer for lazy loading.
Reactive primitives that manage state and trigger updates precisely where they are needed.

list.text: useSignal: Simple value reactivity.
useStore: Deeply reactive objects.

box.content: Qwik serializes the application state and the execution listeners into the HTML.

list.text: Stateful HTML: The UI knows exactly where it left off.
Pure Resumability: No JS execution required until an event occurs.

box.content: Qwik City + Express.js Middleware.
The most mature and widely used Node.js framework, providing a stable foundation for complex APIs.

list.text: Proven: Years of production testing.
Middleware: Access to thousands of existing plugins.

box.content: Integrating Express middleware directly into Qwik City's server-side processing.
Building robust RESTful APIs that serve both your Qwik frontend and external clients.

list.text: Unified: One backend, multiple consumption points.
Reliable: Standard Express routing patterns.

box.content: Centralized error management using Express middleware to ensure consistent API responses.
Route Loaders and Actions in Qwik City.
Functions that run on the server to fetch data before a route is rendered, providing full type safety.
Handles form submissions and data mutations with built-in validation and state management.
Loaders and Actions automatically infer types, ensuring your frontend is always in sync with your backend data.

list.text: Shared Models: Use the same Pydantic/Zod schemas everywhere.
Validated: Catch type errors at compile time.

box.content: Using Express to tunnel requests to microservices while maintaining Qwik's resumability benefits.

list.text: Gateway: Express handles auth and rate limiting.
Streaming: Proxying data efficiently to the client.

box.content: Under the Hood: How Qwik stays small and fast.
A tool that analyzes your code and splits it into tiny, independent 'QRL' chunks.

list.text: Dynamic Splitting: Code is only downloaded when needed.
Tree Shaking: Removing unreachable server code from the client bundle.

box.content: Qwik speculatively fetches the code for components that are likely to be interacted with.

list.text: Predictive: Smart algorithms identify user intent.
Background: Downloads happen during idle time, avoiding lag.

box.content: Code is not only lazy-loaded but also lazy-executed, minimizing the CPU work on the client.
box.code:
// Code stays in the cache until the event fires
const onClick = $(() => { ... });

box.content: Object-Oriented CSS patterns in Qwik ensure that styles are as resumable and optimized as the logic.

list.text: Minimal CSS: Only ship the styles for visible components.
Reusable: Build a component-driven design system with ease.

box.content: Signals, Stores, and Context in Qwik.
Use `useSignal` for primitive values and `useStore` for complex, deeply nested objects.
box.code:
const count = useSignal(0);
const state = useStore({ users: [] });

box.content: Share reactive state across your entire component tree without prop drilling.
box.code:
const AuthContext = createContextId('auth');
const auth = useContext(AuthContext);

box.content: Use `useTask$` and `useVisibleTask$` to handle side effects and data synchronization.

list.text: useTask$: Runs on both server and client.
useVisibleTask$: Runs only on the client when visible.

box.content: Passing signals and stores as props allows child components to react to state changes efficiently.
Tailwind, CSS Modules, and Resumable CSS.
The utility-first framework that fits perfectly into Qwik's component-based architecture.

list.text: Fast: Rapidly build UIs without leaving your HTML.
Consistent: Leverage a robust design system.

box.content: Using `useStylesScpoted$` to ensure styling is encapsulated within your component.
Qwik prefers static CSS or compiled CSS-in-JS to maintain its zero-JS-at-startup guarantee.

list.text: Static: CSS is delivered in the initial HTML.
Clean: No runtime style generation costs.

box.content: Using the `style` prop for dynamic styling while ensuring the optimizer can still do its work.
Adapters, Qwik City, and the Edge.
Deploy your Qwik application anywhere with a single command using built-in adapters.
box.code:
// npx qwik add cloudflare-pages
// npx qwik add netlify-edge

box.content: Leveraging the Edge to handle localized data fetching and security headers before the user's request reaches the origin.

list.text: Localized: Serve users from the nearest data center.
Secure: Perform authentication at the edge.

box.content: Combining SSR, SSG, and CSR to achieve the perfect balance of performance and flexibility.

list.text: Dynamic: SSR for personalized content.
Fast: SSG for static landing pages.

box.content: Using Qwik's strictly-typed environment variables to ensure production configuration is reliable.
Vitest, Playwright, and Mocking.
Fast, native unit tests for your Qwik components and signal logic.
Automating browser interactions to ensure your full-stack flows work perfectly in the real world.

list.text: Reliable: Test complex user journeys across routes.
Visual: Capture screenshots and videos of failures.

box.content: Using tools like MSW (Mock Service Worker) to simulate your Express backend during frontend development and testing.
Verifying that your Express server can handle high traffic volumes and identifying performance bottlenecks.

list.text: Scalable: Ensure your app grows gracefully.
Insights: Monitor CPU and memory usage during stress tests.

box.content: Validation, Logger, and Config.
Using Zod to define schemas that can be used on the server, the client, and within loaders for 100% consistency.
box.code:
// shared/schemas.ts
export const UserSchema = z.object({ id: z.string() });

box.content: Integrating Morgan or Winston into your Express server for detailed request and error logging.

list.text: Traceable: Debug issues faster with clear logs.
Analyzable: Export logs to systems like ELK or Datadog.

box.content: A centralized approach to managing secrets and configurations across the full stack.
Using TypeScript utility types (`Pick`, `Omit`, `Partial`) to strictly define data shapes for different layers of your app.

list.text: Modular: Types evolve with your business logic.
Strong: Prevent accidental data leakages.

box.content: Mastering the Resumable Stack.
Qwik and Express provide a unique combination of cutting-edge resumability and industry-standard backend reliability.

list.text: Resumability: The next step in web performance.
Express: The solid foundation for every project.
Tooling: Exceptional DX with Qwik City and Node.js.

box.content: Keep an eye on the continued evolution of resumability and the move towards more specialized edge computing runtimes.

list.text: Growth: Qwik is quickly gaining a loyal following.
Optimization: Expect even smaller bundles and faster delivery.

box.content: Resumability is not just a feature; it's a paradigm shift. Start building with Qwik and Express today!
Thank you for your time! Any questions?
